title: BP Exits Major Australian Green Hydrogen and Renewables Project in Strategic Reset
content: Oil and gas giant BP has withdrawn from plans to develop a large-scale renewable and green hydrogen hub in Pilbara, Australia, which had aimed to generate up to 26GW of renewable power capacity. The hub was set to produce significant quantities of green hydrogen and ammonia for both domestic and export markets. In 2022, BP announced it would take a 40.5% stake and operatorship of the AREH project in Western Australia (WA). Following BPâ€™s exit, a spokesperson said, â€œThis decision reflects BPâ€™s recent strategy reset, which involves growing BPâ€™s upstream oil and gas business, focusing its downstream business, and investing with increasing discipline into the transition.â€
author: Analysts Account
